subject: exfat: remove exfat_config.h messing kernel config.
author: Sorgelig

impact.effect_if_absent: No effect on current 6.18 build; commit applies only to fork's Samsung exfat driver which is not used in 6.18. Vanilla exfat (used by current build) has different structure and never had exfat_config.h.

license_provenance: GPL-2.0+, Copyright (C) 2012-2013 Samsung Electronics Co., Ltd. (from exfat_config.h header)
notes: This commit is part of the fork's Samsung exfat driver development (one of 6 commits in patch-provenance.md §3.8/§N1 that replace mainline exfat with out-of-tree Samsung driver). The commit removes exfat_config.h (69 lines of compile-time defaults) and updates 18 source files to remove includes of it, plus changes one preprocessor directive from '#if CONFIG_EXFAT_DEBUG_MSG' to '#ifdef CONFIG_EXFAT_DEBUG_MSG' in exfat_core.h. Since CONFIG_* symbols are properly defined in fs/exfat/Kconfig, the driver still compiles. HOWEVER: Our 6.18 build uses vanilla exfat (ADR 0010 decision, amended by ADR 0019), not the Samsung driver. Vanilla exfat has completely different file structure (balloc.c, cache.c, dir.c, etc. vs exfat_core.c, exfat_super.c, etc.) and never had exfat_config.h. This commit is dropped via architecture decision (ADR 0010/0019 chose vanilla exfat + carried patch 0031 for Samsung-compatible symlink support), not via technical incompatibility. Patch-provenance.md groups this with 5 sibling commits (8b6b8c2f5, df35bdb27, 858322ce6, 5220d6686, 99a2c80d0) but worker instructions require analyzing this commit in isolation. All 6 commits are part of the Samsung driver replacement sequence, all 6 are dropped together by ADR decision. sonnet-verified: disposition=dropped-deliberate and severity=none confirmed correct; equivalence=null appropriately left unset.
